title: Trump threatens Iran with escalation, including strikes on civilian and nuclear infrastructure
subhed: After two weeks of strikes on Iran that yielded no progress, US President Donald Trump raised the possibility of targeting uranium enrichment sites beneath the Kolang mountain in central Iran.

EU struggles to adopt new round of economic sanctions against Russia
subhed: The 27 member states reached an agreement on Thursday on a 21st package of sanctions against Moscow. Several member states asserted their individual interests and secured concessions.

title: Fire ravages forest in southwestern France as thousands evacuated
subhed: Thousands have been evacuated from a town in Gironde in southwestern France after a fast-moving fire broke out on Wednesday.

title: ''No kids' policies promote a polarized and reductive vision of society'
subhed: As child-free spaces continue to grow rapidly, a group of non-profit leaders, researchers and artists argue for Le Monde that the youngest members of society should not be seen as a nuisance to be tolerated, but as a valuable presence to be welcomed.

title: NGOs appeal to French state to stop economic support for Israel's occupation of Palestine
subhed: Five NGOs filed a legal appeal to require the French government to prevent any economic activity that might support Israel's settlement of Palestinian territories. The unprecedented legal move is based on the International Court of Justice's 2024 advisory opinion.

subhed: France has decided to ban those under 15 from major platforms such as Instagram and TikTok. The country is following a global trend that has gained momentum in recent years.

title: 20 men, a 'sperm party,' and two days of sexual torment: The brutality of the 'libertine' rapes case
subhed: Over the course of a long weekend, one of Christophe B.'s former partners was forced to endure sexual assaults by numerous men at their home. Some of these assaults were broadcast live on the swinging website Wyylde.

title: How Bernard Arnault works to keep his media outlets in line
subhed: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (4/6). Since the 1990s, the CEO of LVMH has built a small media galaxy in print and radio, which he uses to convey his ideas and tastes, but also to promote his brands.
